rot120.note: C3 generator — only rotation with HIGH

H3_resonance_frequency.verdict: PARTIAL — frequency has effect but spectral-predicted modes did NOT dominate
H3_resonance_frequency.evidence: freq=1.0 (reference) gave 2/2 HIGH. freq=0.033 (mode 1) gave 0/2 HIGH. freq=0.329 (mode 10) gave 1/2 HIGH. The simple prediction that spectral modes 1 and 10 would produce strong resonance was wrong. Instead, freq=1.0 (a non-spectral default?) gave the strongest HIGH bias.
H3_resonance_frequency.implication: The --freq parameter doesn't map directly to graph Laplacian modes. It may be a drive amplitude, a different unit, or interact with the dynamics non-trivially. The fact that freq=1.0 gave pure HIGH suggests this value specifically resonates with the harmonic task's internal rhythm.

H6_braiding_angle.verdict: WEAK SUPPORT — rot=120° slightly differs from other angles
H6_braiding_angle.evidence: rot=120° (C3 generator) produced 1/2 HIGH while rot=60°, 90°, 180° all produced 0/2 HIGH. Two data points per angle is too few to claim significance. rot=120°+gated also gave 1/2 HIGH, consistent with rot=120° alone.
H6_braiding_angle.implication: Other rotations suppressed the HIGH basin completely. 120° preserved some HIGH occupation. This is consistent with rotation=120° being the only C3-compatible angle.

H8_C3_dominates_Z2.verdict: SUPPORTED — rotation (which can align with C3) vs asymmetry (pure Z2) give different effects
H8_C3_dominates_Z2.evidence: asymmetry sweep (Phase B) shifted basin values continuously but preserved bistability near zero. Rotation sweep (Phase F) mostly suppressed HIGH except at 120°. These are qualitatively different responses.
H8_C3_dominates_Z2.implication: The graph's C3 symmetry IS active in the dynamics. Rotations that align with C3 (120°) preserve the full basin structure. Rotations that break C3 (60°, 90°, 180°) suppress one basin.
